steps.title: Set up the ratio as an equation
steps.content: The ratio of t to u is 2 to 3, which means: $$\frac{t}{u} = \frac{2}{3}$$

steps.title: Substitute t = 18 and solve for u
steps.content: Substitute $t = 18$ into the equation: $$\frac{18}{u} = \frac{2}{3}$$ Cross-multiply to isolate u: $$18 \cdot 3 = 2 \cdot u$$ $$54 = 2u$$ $$27 = u$$ That confirms D is correct.
